title: Looking Back Together
description: Adachi and Shimamura watch old video clips of their high school days in a mysterious room. A nostalgic, tender moment of reflection on how much they've changed—and how much they haven't.
content:
Adachi laughed at my comment. 'You've really got the nerve to say that.'

'Just some bread and water for me. Please~'

'Still… you know, in the strangest ways, our personalities really do match, Shimamura.'

As she watched the old video clips, Adachi's fingers twitched every time her past self opened her mouth.

'Wh-who is that person?'

'…That's you.'

Unable to face her younger self, she slid right off the pale-blue sofa and curled up on the carpet like a pill bug. Every so often she kicked her legs in embarrassment, which only made the whole thing funnier.

'A-and I'm even standing next to some blonde girl I don't know…'

'That's me.'

Seeing my blonde self on the screen, I could tell how much baby fat I still had. We were just living out our high school days, yet looking back, the years felt heavier than I expected. What have I gained in those two years? And what had quietly slipped away?

Meanwhile, Adachi was rolling around at my feet like she was trying to fight off an emotion she couldn't name.

'Okay, Adachi. Time to revive.'

I pulled her up by the wrist. She reluctantly got onto her knees, stole a half-second glance at the screen, then instantly turned away—ears bright red, as always.

'I just… I'm so embarrassed…'

'You are?'

'My personality back then was completely different…'

'Wahahaha.'

I'd said it before, but seeing it again made it painfully obvious how much she'd changed. The girl on the screen smiled brightly—cheerful, a little sarcastic, but natural—and spoke to me like it was the easiest thing in the world.

'Who even is that…? Th-that can't be me…'

'You're not bad enough to deny your whole past.'

'Look, look—' I pointed at the screen, but Adachi scrambled back onto the sofa and knelt with her back rigidly turned toward it.

'O-okay, enough! I'm done watching!'

'We've already made it this far. Might as well keep going.'

The video played on. Hino and Nagafuji appeared on the second floor of the gym. While the footage ran, I glanced over. Adachi turned toward me—still kneeling—and gave me this wounded, pleading look.

The room itself felt off: walls, ceiling, and even the sofa were the same soft blue. A long rectangular space with nothing but a massive screen and the sofa. Light filtered through the window, hazy and weak, as if it faded before reaching us. It didn't feel like a real room at all.

Apparently this strange place was part of a 'special project.'

'You know… the past is easier to deal with if you don't stare at every little detail.'

Like passing a store you used to love. From far away, it's nostalgic. Up close, you notice every scratch and crack—and sometimes it stings, just like it did for Adachi. But maybe it was that sting that finally pushed her to whisper the truth.

'I always… often… sometimes think about you, Shimamura.'

'…Now you're making me embarrassed.'

'Sometimes, when a memory pops up out of nowhere, the first thing I see is the blonde you.'

'……………'

On the screen, the younger me stretched her arms and ran around like an airplane.

'That must've been… the first version of you I ever held in my heart.'

Her quiet voice folded inward and sank into me.

'…Mm.'

I lifted a strand of my black hair and rubbed it between my fingers. From blonde to black—my hair was basically a timeline of who I'd been.

But the Adachi I met back then… she was the same Adachi sitting here now. Changing constantly. And honestly, watching her change has become one of my favorite hobbies.

'But, uh… how exactly do we get out of this room?'

'…Huh?'

We'd wandered in without thinking, but there was no door.

'Maybe we can't leave until the video ends?'

I pointed at the screen. It showed Adachi sitting up in bed, face blazing red. She looked exactly the same as always—until subtitles suddenly appeared beneath her.

'…Uh, what's this? 'I had a dream about kissing Shimamura…''

'W–WAAAH!!'

Adachi launched at me like a wild animal, covering my eyes as she tackled me off the sofa. We rolled across the carpet together. Even without seeing the screen, the warmth and noise of the moment blurred past and present into one bright, chaotic feeling—and somehow, it was beautiful.
